topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias sat hunched over his worn acoustic guitar, the familiar melody a comforting shield against the encroaching silence of his cramped apartment. He was tuning up, lost in the intricate fingerpicking pattern of his latest composition, when it happened. A sound drifted through the thin walls—a rendition of his song, played with uncanny accuracy, yet infused with a raw, almost predatory energy. His fingers froze mid-chord. He slowly lowered the guitar, his breath catching in his throat. Who could be playing it? The air in the room suddenly felt thick, heavy, pressing down on his chest. He stood up, his legs feeling strangely unsteady, and crept toward the door, his heart hammering a frantic rhythm against his ribs. He didn't dare look through the peephole; he just needed to know.

[story 2]

The studio was supposed to be his sanctuary, a place where his music lived, but tonight, it felt like a trap. He was reviewing the final track when the sound bled through the closed door—his song, transposed, reinterpreted, by someone whose voice carried an unsettling, knowing timbre. A cold sweat broke out on his palms. He gripped the edge of the mixing desk until his knuckles turned white, his gaze darting toward the soundproofing panels as if expecting an intruder to materialize from the foam. Every nerve ending seemed hyper-alert. He backed away slowly, his movements jerky, a desperate urge to flee overriding his desire to stay and confront the source of the intrusion.

[story 3]

Maya was sketching lyrics in her notebook, trying to recapture the elusive feeling of the song she’d written. Then, it started. A guitar riff, familiar yet utterly alien, weaving through the quiet street noise. It was her tune, performed by a stranger, someone whose execution was flawless, yet somehow chillingly intimate. Maya dropped her pencil. The small metallic clatter echoed loudly in the stillness. Her skin prickled, a sudden, intense awareness of every shadow in the room. She stood up abruptly, her eyes wide, scanning the darkened windows. A tremor ran through her hands, making it difficult to steady herself as she moved toward the window, needing to see, needing to understand what she was witnessing.
